devdoc.author: fhevm-hardhat-template
devdoc.details: Both pools and both per-user stakes update on every bet (the non-chosen side adds an encrypted zero) so the side cannot be inferred from which storage slot changed.

params.bettor: The address that placed the bet.

params.question: The market question.
params.resolveDeadline: Unix timestamp separating betting from resolution.
params.resolver: The address allowed to resolve the market.

params.winningPool: The pool on the winning side.

bet(uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes).details: Both pools and both user stakes are updated every call; the unchosen side adds an encrypted zero so the chosen side is not observable from storage writes. User decrypt rights on stakes are intentionally deferred to claim() to prevent mid-market side inference from which stake handle a user can decrypt.

params.amount: Encrypted stake amount (externalEuint64) from the client.
params.inputProof: Shared input proof covering both `amount` and `side` (one encrypt() bundle).

claim(uint256).details: Uses plaintext-divisor FHE.div (winningPool is revealed), which is why pools must be finalized first. If winningPool is 0 (no winners) payouts are 0. Idempotency is enforced per caller; the handle is also readable later via getPayout().

returns.payout: The encrypted payout handle, decryptable by the caller.

params.question: Human-readable market question.
params.resolveDeadline: Unix timestamp; must be strictly in the future. Bets accepted strictly before it, resolution after it.

finalizePools(uint256,bytes,bytes).details: `abiEncodedCleartexts` and `decryptionProof` come verbatim from the relayer SDK `publicDecrypt([yesPool, noPool])` call. Decoding mirrors the [yes, no] handle order.

params.abiEncodedCleartexts: ABI-encoded cleartexts as returned by the SDK.
params.decryptionProof: KMS signatures returned by the SDK.

returns.noStake: The caller's encrypted NO stake handle.
returns.yesStake: The caller's encrypted YES stake handle.

params.winningSide: true = YES won, false = NO won.

devdoc.title: Confidential Prediction Market

InvalidKMSSignatures().notice: Returned if the returned KMS signatures are not valid.

SenderNotAllowedToUseHandle(bytes32,addr.notice: Returned if the sender is not allowed to use the handle.

ZamaProtocolUnsupported().notice: Returned if the Zama protocol is not supported on the current chain

BetPlaced(uint256,address).notice: Emitted when a bet is placed.

Claimed(uint256,address).notice: Emitted when a bettor claims their payout.

MarketCreated(uint256,address,string,uin.notice: Emitted when a market is opened.

MarketResolved(uint256,bool).notice: Emitted when the resolver declares the outcome.

PoolsFinalized(uint256,uint64,uint64).notice: Emitted when the revealed pools are verified and stored.

PublicDecryptionVerified(bytes32[],bytes.notice: This event is emitted when public decryption has been successfully verified.

bet(uint256,bytes32,bytes32,bytes).notice: Places an encrypted bet. Both the amount and the side are ciphertext inputs.

claim(uint256).notice: Computes the caller's encrypted payout for a finalized market and grants the caller decryption rights over it. Payout = stake_on_winning_side * totalPool / winningPool.

createMarket(string,uint64).notice: Opens a new binary market. Caller becomes the resolver.

finalizePools(uint256,bytes,bytes).notice: Submits the publicly decrypted pool totals plus the KMS decryption proof. The proof is verified against the pool handles (order: [yesPool, noPool]); on success the plaintext total/winning pools are stored, enabling claims.

getPayout(uint256).notice: Returns the caller's encrypted payout handle for a claimed market.

getPools(uint256).notice: Returns the encrypted pool handles (decryptable only after resolution).

getStakes(uint256).notice: Returns the caller's encrypted stake handles for a market.

marketCount().notice: Number of markets created; valid market ids are [0, marketCount).

resolve(uint256,bool).notice: Resolver declares the plaintext winning side and exposes the pools for public decryption. The pool ciphertexts are flagged decryptable here; the cleartexts are submitted back through finalizePools().

userdoc.notice: Binary (YES/NO) markets where each user's stake amount and chosen side stay encrypted on-chain until the market resolves. Only the aggregate pools are revealed (at resolution) so payouts can be computed with a plaintext divisor; individual bets remain private. This is an "encrypted accounting" build: no funding token moves, the pools and positions are encrypted ledgers and payouts are decryptable figures.
